 {"id":"bd-19k","title":"MVCC: Implement MVCC-aware block device wrapper (read snapshot -> version or base)","description":"Goal: make block reads/writes snapshot-aware.

Deliverables:
- Define MVCCBlockDevice (or similar) that wraps an underlying BlockDevice plus a version store.
- Read path: read_visible(block, snapshot) else fall back to base device read_block().
- Write path: stage_write(block, bytes) into the current transaction.
- Ensure block buffers are block_size aligned and validated.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests demonstrate snapshot visibility with base+versioned reads.
- No global locks on read path; concurrency model documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:33.557055409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:13:45.798997520Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:13:45.798977844Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19k","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:23:53.105838366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19k","depends_on_id":"bd-2fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:23:53.024630399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a9","title":"ext4: Implement feature flag decoding + validation policy (compat/ro_compat/incompat)","description":"Goal: represent ext4 feature flags in a way that is (1) easy to validate, (2) easy to print for UX, and (3) safe against unknown bits.

Deliverables:
- Define bitflags (or equivalent) for compat/ro_compat/incompat.
- Implement decode helpers that produce:
  - required bits missing?
  - explicitly rejected bits present?
  - unknown incompatible bits present?
- v1 mount policy:
  - require FILETYPE + EXTENTS
  - block sizes limited to 1K/2K/4K
  - reject compression/encrypt/casefold/inline-data, etc.

Acceptance:
- validate_v1() returns ParseError with stable field+reason.
- Tests cover: required bits missing, unknown incompat bit, known rejected bit, allowed-but-nonrequired bits.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:56.882710351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:10:51.691798163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:10:51.691780019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a9","depends_on_id":"bd-2cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.539865706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1aid","title":"Fix persistent MVCC commit error semantics: rollback on WAL encode failure and return non-conflict durability errors","description":"In crates/ffs-mvcc/src/persist.rs, commit_internal currently commits in-memory before WAL encoding, and on wal::encode_commit failure it returns CommitError::Conflict { block: 0, ... } without rollback. WAL append/sync failures are also surfaced as fake FCW conflicts. Implement proper rollback for encode failure and introduce a dedicated commit error variant for durability/persistence failures so callers can distinguish I/O durability failure from write-write conflict. Update relevant mappings/tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:22:06.204515744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:42:28.129150501Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:42:28.129120805Z","close_reason":"Added dedicated durability commit error, rollback on WAL encode failure, and updated mappings/tests to avoid fake FCW conflicts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","mvcc","wal"]}

Make skip outcomes explicit and ensure CI does not report false-green when required tools/images are unavailable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:56:33.481340139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T21:56:40.591247577Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1b7","title":"Implement btrfs COW B-tree mutation (insert/delete/split/merge)","description":"# Implement btrfs COW B-tree mutation (insert/delete/split/merge)

## GOAL
Implement the core btrfs COW B-tree mutation operations required for write path.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs uses COW semantics for all tree modifications:
- Never modify existing nodes in place
- Create new nodes with modifications
- Update parent pointers up to root
- Old root becomes part of transaction log

This is naturally compatible with MVCC version chains.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Core Operations
```rust
pub trait BtrfsBTree {
    /// Insert key-value pair, returns new root
    fn insert(&self, key: BtrfsKey, item: &[u8], cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Delete key, returns new root (or same if not found)
    fn delete(&self, key: &BtrfsKey, cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Update existing key with new value
    fn update(&self, key: &BtrfsKey, item: &[u8], cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Range query
    fn range(&self, start: &BtrfsKey, end: &BtrfsKey, cx: &Cx) 
        -> Result<Vec<(BtrfsKey, Vec<u8>)>>;
}
```

### 2. COW Mechanics
```rust
/// Clone-on-write a node with modification
fn cow_node(
    original: BlockNumber,
    modification: NodeMod,
    allocator: &dyn BtrfsAllocator,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<BlockNumber> {
    // 1. Read original node
    // 2. Apply modification (insert/delete/update item)
    // 3. Allocate new block
    // 4. Write modified node to new block
    // 5. Return new block number
}
```

### 3. Split/Merge
- Split when node is full (> max items)
- Merge when node is underfull (< min items)
- Rebalance siblings before merge

### 4. Path Walking
- Track path from root to leaf for COW propagation
- Each level gets new block on modification
- Efficient path reuse for sequential inserts

## TESTS
1. Unit: Insert into empty tree
2. Unit: Insert causing split
3. Unit: Delete causing merge
4. Unit: COW produces new root
5. Integration: 1000 random inserts/deletes, tree invariants hold
6. Property: Random operations maintain sorted order

## LOGGING
- Tree modification (trace): operation, key, old root, new root
- Split/merge events (debug): node level, item counts
- Allocation (trace): new block numbers

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All operations preserve B-tree invariants
2. COW produces independent tree versions
3. No in-place modification of shared blocks
4. Performance: O(log N) for all operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:23.658366987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:18.160085044Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:18.160001627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","btrfs","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1b7","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:08.481147860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1b7","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.683503676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1b7","depends_on_id":"bd-375.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:08.388968210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":48,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BTRFS COW SEMANTICS: btrfs B-tree mutation is inherently COW—every modification creates a new node and updates the parent's pointer. This propagates up to the root, which gets a new generation number. This is fundamentally different from ext4 extent trees (in-place mutation + journal). The COW chain must be atomic: either all nodes in the chain are updated, or none are.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:59Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added in-memory COW btrfs mutation core in crates/ffs-btrfs (BtrfsBTree trait + InMemoryCowBtrfsTree with insert/update/delete/range, split logic, root COW propagation, invariant validator, and deterministic random mutation test). Added tracing instrumentation for insert/split/delete paths. Full required gates pass (fmt/check/clippy/test workspace). Remaining gap before closure: optimize delete path from rebuild-based fallback to strict O(log N) merge/rebalance semantics and integrate allocator-backed block lifecycle for production write path wiring.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:36Z"},{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation note: full workspace gates currently blocked by concurrent non-bd-1b7 compile errors in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs (E0502 at line ~6190, E0133 at line ~6412) and warning in crates/ffs-mvcc/src/sharded.rs. Package-local gates for ffs-btrfs pass (fmt, test, clippy). Awaiting coordination on cross-bead workspace breakage before closure.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:02:37Z"},{"id":61,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Pausing active work to pick next high-impact bead per bv robot triage. Current implementation is in place; remaining closure blocker is full-workspace gate failure from concurrent non-bd-1b7 edits (ffs-core/ffs-mvcc).","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:04:14Z"},{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed from bv robot-next and started closure triage.

Current state from code audit (crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs):
- Delete path is already strict O(log N): delete_from + rebalance_child + rotate_from_left/right + merge_adjacent_nodes (no tree rebuild fallback in current implementation).
- Primary remaining closure gap is allocator-backed block lifecycle integration:
  - alloc_node currently only bumps in-memory next_block and inserts cloned nodes into nodes map.
  - No corresponding allocator-backed free path for superseded COW nodes.

Coordination status:
- Reservation conflict on crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs (holder: BrightForge, reservation id 21, expires ~09:32Z).
- Sent coordination message in Agent Mail thread bd-1b7; will proceed with allocator lifecycle implementation once reservation is released/handed off.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:04Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-1b7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed allocator-backed COW node lifecycle integration in crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs.

Implemented:
- Added `BtrfsAllocator` lifecycle interface and default `InMemoryBtrfsAllocator`.
- `InMemoryCowBtrfsTree` now supports custom allocator injection (`with_allocator`) and records deferred frees.
- Wired allocation through allocator (`alloc_node`) and deferred-free tracking (`retire_node`).
- Added deferred-free lifecycle hooks through mutation flow:
  - `insert_into` retires replaced node IDs.
  - `delete_from` retires replaced leaf/internal nodes.
  - `rebalance_child` retires replaced/merged sibling nodes.
  - `normalize_root_after_delete` retires collapsed root nodes.
- Added regression test: `cow_mutations_record_deferred_node_frees`.

Validation:
- cargo fmt --check PASS
- cargo check --all-targets PASS
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS
- cargo test --workspace PASS

Closure note:
- Prior bead note cited delete-path fallback as a remaining concern; current implementation already uses O(log N) rebalance/merge, and this change closes the allocator lifecycle gap.","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1bx","title":"Implement degradation level display in TUI dashboard","description":"# Implement degradation level display in TUI dashboard

## GOAL
Add degradation level and pressure indicators to the TUI dashboard so operators can monitor system health.

## BACKGROUND
The graceful degradation system (bd-3tz) produces state that must be visible:
- Current degradation level (0-4)
- CPU/memory/IO pressure gauges
- Recent degradation events

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Extended DashboardSnapshot
```rust
pub struct DashboardSnapshot {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // Degradation state
    pub degradation_level: DegradationLevel,
    pub cpu_pressure: f64,      // 0.0 - 1.0
    pub memory_pressure: f64,   // 0.0 - 1.0
    pub io_queue_depth: usize,
    pub degradation_events: Vec<DegradationEvent>,
}
```

### 2. New Panel: System Health
```
┌─ System Health ─────────────────┐
│ Degradation: NORMAL (Level 0)   │
│                                 │
│ CPU:    [████████░░] 78%        │
│ Memory: [██████░░░░] 62%        │
│ I/O:    [███░░░░░░░] 34%        │
│                                 │
│ Recent events:                  │
│   12:34:05 Level 0 → 1 (CPU)    │
│   12:35:22 Level 1 → 0          │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3. Color Coding
- Level 0 (Normal): Green
- Level 1 (Background paused): Yellow
- Level 2 (Cache reduced): Orange
- Level 3 (Writes throttled): Red
- Level 4 (Read-only): Blinking Red

### 4. Gauge Widgets
```rust
fn render_pressure_gauge(value: f64, width: u16) -> String {
    let filled = (value * width as f64) as u16;
    let bar = "█".repeat(filled as usize) + &"░".repeat((width - filled) as usize);
    format!("[{}] {:>3}%", bar, (value * 100.0) as u8)
}
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Gauge rendering at various values
2. Unit: Color coding for each level
3. Integration: Dashboard updates on level change
4. Visual: Screenshot test for layout

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Degradation level always visible
2. Pressure gauges update in real-time
3. Event history shows last 10 events
4. Colors distinguish severity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:53:15.911738202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T18:07:11.355345067Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T18:07:11.355313619Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","reliability","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bx","depends_on_id":"bd-1ymg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:30.032294380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1bx","depends_on_id":"bd-2r8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:18.469715373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1bx","depends_on_id":"bd-3rix","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.580622126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1bx","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:38.580793966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-1bx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:30Z"}]}
